frame_analyses.description: Yellow foam Penrose drain visible on block with marked entry/exit points. Needle driver (green handle) approaching needle with appropriate grasp at approximately 2/3 point from tip.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grasp position, driver oriented for controlled entry

frame_analyses.description: Needle driving through drain at entry mark with left hand (green driver) leading. Entry angle appears perpendicular to drain slit.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean perpendicular entry, good control of needle trajectory

frame_analyses.description: Needle fully driven through drain, exiting near marked exit point. Suture loop forming above drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle passage, exit point appears aligned with mark

frame_analyses.description: Right hand (bare hand visible) wrapping suture around left needle driver for first throw. Wrapping motion indicates double throw (surgeon's knot).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right hand leading first throw, wrapping appears complete for double throw

frame_analyses.description: First throw progressing, suture wrapped around left driver twice (visible wrap pattern), forming surgeon's knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean double throw formation, appropriate tension management

frame_analyses.description: First knot (surgeon's knot) being tightened and seated on drain. Knot appears secure with appropriate tension.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot cinching smoothly, no slippage observed

frame_analyses.description: Left hand (green driver) now leading wrap around right driver for second throw. Hand switch from throw 1 confirmed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear hand switch to left lead, single wrap visible (single throw)

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being seated, single wrap completing around right driver. Knot cinching onto drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper single throw execution, hand switch maintained

frame_analyses.description: Right hand again leading wrap around left driver for third throw. Hand switch from second throw confirmed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Correct hand alternation, single wrap forming

frame_analyses.description: Third throw being seated and cinched. Knot stack visible on drain, all three throws appear secure.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean knot sequence completion, appropriate tension throughout

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic scissors positioned to cut first suture end near knot. Scissors fully visible with green handle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good scissors grasp and positioning for suture cut

frame_analyses.description: First suture end cut, second suture end being positioned for cutting. Drain shows completed closure with three-throw knot stack.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean first cut, second end ready for cutting

frame_analyses.description: Second suture end cut inside trainer. Task complete. Final drain appearance shows intact closure with knot stack seated on marked closure line.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both suture ends successfully cut intracorporeally. Drain slit appears fully closed with no visible gap.

penalties.description: Entry point approximately 1mm lateral to marked entry point on drain
Exit point approximately 1.5mm distal to marked exit point

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double wrap around left driver, appropriate tension, knot seated firmly on drain
Left hand correctly switched to leading position, single wrap executed cleanly
Right hand correctly switched back to leading position, single throw secure

drain_assessment.notes: Drain slit appears fully approximated by final knot stack, no visible gaps or tissue damage

hand_switching.notes: All required hand switches performed correctly between throws

confidence_rationale: Completion time and phase execution clearly observable throughout video. Knot throw sequence and hand switching well-documented with clear frame evidence. Suture deviation measurements estimated from oblique camera angles at entry/exit points, creating modest uncertainty (±1mm). Drain closure assessment highly confident with clear final frame showing complete slit approximation. No ambiguity regarding task completion or knot security.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter deviation at entry and exit marks — camera angle is oblique to drain slit, limiting precision measurement to ±1mm estimate
technique_summary: Excellent expert-level intracorporeal suturing performance. Task completed in 65 seconds (well below 112s proficiency target). Clean needle passage with minimal deviation, textbook surgeon's knot formation, correct hand switching through all three throws, secure knot stack, and complete drain closure with no visible gaps. Minor technical deviations from marked points are within acceptable range. Final knot integrity appears excellent.
strengths: Rapid needle loading and suture placement (8.5s combined vs 13s expert benchmark)
Proper double-throw surgeon's knot formation with appropriate tension
Consistent hand switching across all three throws as required
Clean suture-cut execution with both ends removed intracorporeally
Complete drain slit closure with no visible gap or tissue damage
improvement_suggestions: Refine entry angle to achieve perpendicular needle passage and reduce entry deviation slightly below 1mm
Consider marking alignment technique to ensure exit point matches marked target more precisely
